name: multi_subst_register_renames
title: Plain register-renaming substs from gen_regfix
summary: Most common NEAR_MISS shape: GCC picked different registers than the original at multiple sites, but the C structure is otherwise identical. `dc.sh gen-regfix <func>` enumerates the substs needed; we apply them all and the build matches.
proof: func_8005B7C4 (commit 7580e1d, 2 substs), func_8007C748 (commit 1c275b6, 13 substs across 9 indices).

structural: the diff is dominated by 'register substs' rules with no instruction_swaps
no 3-cycle: no single index has multiple subst rules with both different patterns AND different replacements

rule_skeleton.comment: apply all register substs from gen_regfix verbatim

notes: Run `dc.sh gen-regfix <func>` and pipe the output into regfix.txt verbatim, OR use `dc.sh near-miss <func> --apply` for the automated try-and-revert flow.
If gen_regfix reports a 3-cycle (multiple substs at the same index with different patterns AND replacements), use the `3cycle_full_insn_subst` recipe instead -- simple substs can't express the cycle.
Watch for `feedback_subst_count1_gotcha.md`: regfix subst replaces only the FIRST match per index. If a register appears twice on the same instruction, you may need two substs at that index.
